title: Mixed Graph: The charts show the average number of hours worked per week and the proportions ...
description: The charts show the average number of hours worked per week and the proportions of people suffering from stress-related illness across eight
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1

1. Dạng bài

  • Mixed charts: Bar chart \+ Pie chart
  • Nội dung: working hours \+ stress levels by occupation

2. Introduction (công thức)

  • bar chart \+ pie chart \+ working hours \+ stress \+ occupations

3. Overview (ý chính, không số)

  • Giờ làm việc: chênh lệch lớn giữa các nghề
  • Business men: làm nhiều nhất
  • Lecturers: làm ít nhất
  • Stress: lawyers cao nhất
  • Programmers: thấp nhất
  • Không phải nghề làm nhiều giờ nhất sẽ stress cao nhất

4. Body 1 – Bar chart (Working hours)

Viết theo ranking

  • cao nhất: business men
  • tiếp theo: movie producers, doctors
  • trung bình: writers, programmers, lawyers
  • thấp: chefs
  • thấp nhất: lecturers

→ Key idea:

  • sự khác biệt rõ rệt về số giờ làm

5. Body 2 – Pie chart (Stress levels)

Viết theo tỷ lệ

  • cao nhất: lawyers
  • tiếp theo: movie producers
  • trung bình: doctors, business men, chefs
  • thấp: writers, lecturers
  • thấp nhất: programmers

→ Key idea:

  • stress không hoàn toàn phụ thuộc vào giờ làm

6. Từ vựng cần dùng

  • rank first / second
  • account for
  • the highest / the lowest
  • considerably higher than
  • moderate level
  • in contrast

The charts compare the average weekly working hours of eight professions and the proportion of stress-related illness among people in these occupations.

Overall, business men and movie producers worked the longest hours, while lecturers and chefs had the shortest working weeks. In terms of stress, lecturers accounted for the largest share, whereas programmers experienced the lowest levels.

Business men worked the most, averaging 70 hours per week, followed by movie producers at 60 hours. Doctors also had relatively long working weeks at 52 hours, while writers worked 45 hours and programmers 40. Lawyers averaged 35 hours, and the figures were lower for chefs and lecturers, at 30 and 25 hours respectively.

Regarding stress-related illness, lecturers represented the highest proportion at 25%, despite having the shortest working hours. Movie producers followed at 18%, while doctors accounted for 15%. Business men made up 11% of stress cases, and chefs 10%. Writers and lawyers each contributed 8%, whereas programmers had the lowest figure at 5%.
